metadata.name: meridian
metadata.description: Meridian on-chain CLOB for binary outcome markets — Anchor program wrapping a pure-Rust matching engine.

docs: U3 wired `initialize_config` and `create_strike_market`. U4 adds
`mint_pair` and `burn_pair`. U5 adds `place_limit_order`,
`place_market_order`, `cancel_order`. U6 adds `buy_no` and
`sell_no` — the atomic single-tx Buy-No / Sell-No trade paths.
U7 adds the remaining instructions (settle/redeem).

instructions.name: admin_settle_market
docs: Admin-only emergency settlement (P1 stuck-oracle deadlock). Stamps
`yes_wins`/!`yes_wins` as the outcome WITHOUT reading Pyth, but only
after `expiry + EMERGENCY_GRACE_SECONDS` (24h) so normal oracle
settlement always gets first claim. Solvent by the $1 invariant.

accounts.name: admin
docs: Admin authority. Must equal `config.admin`.

accounts.name: config
docs: Singleton Config — authenticates the admin. Boxed for stack hygiene
(same reason as `settle_market`).

accounts.name: market
docs: Market to force-settle. Mutated.

instructions.name: burn_pair
docs: Burn `amount` Yes + `amount` No from the caller and return `amount`
USDC from the per-market escrow. The symmetric inverse of `mint_pair`.

accounts.name: user
docs: Caller — pays Yes + No, receives USDC.

accounts.name: config
docs: Global config — read-only here; we only need the pause flag + the
pinned USDC mint for the user's source/escrow constraints.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market this burn pair belongs to. Yes/No mints and the mint-auth
PDA are validated against fields on this account.

accounts.name: user_usdc
docs: User's USDC ATA — receives the returned `amount` USDC.

accounts.name: usdc_escrow
docs: USDC escrow PDA owned by the market's mint-authority PDA. Source of
the returned USDC.

accounts.name: yes_mint
docs: Yes mint — supply decreases by `amount`.

accounts.name: no_mint
docs: No mint — supply decreases by `amount`.

accounts.name: user_yes
docs: User's Yes ATA. Constraint requires balance ≥ amount — surfaces the
shortfall as a clean Anchor error rather than letting the burn CPI
fail deep in the token program.

accounts.name: user_no
docs: User's No ATA. Same balance constraint as `user_yes`.

accounts.name: mint_authority
docs: the USDC escrow → user transfer.

instructions.name: buy_no
docs: Atomic "Buy No" — mint a Yes/No pair against USDC, then market-sell
the Yes leg in the same tx. User ends holding `amount` No tokens
+ USDC proceeds from the Yes sale. Reverts atomically if the Yes
sell leg can't fill the full `amount` within `min_yes_sell_price`.

accounts.name: user
docs: Caller. Pays USDC, ends holding No tokens.

accounts.name: config
docs: Global config — paused flag + canonical USDC mint.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market the trade is against. `has_one` checks pin the Yes / No
mints stored on `Market` to the accounts the user passes.

accounts.name: book
docs: Zero-copy `Book` account for the market. Mutated by the market-sell
leg via `place_order_inner`.

accounts.name: usdc_escrow
docs: USDC escrow PDA — receives the `amount` USDC from `mint_pair_inner`,
sources the per-fill USDC payouts to makers in `place_order_inner`.

accounts.name: yes_escrow
docs: Yes escrow PDA — receives the minted Yes from the taker's
up-front lock in `place_order_inner`, sourced out to makers per fill.

accounts.name: yes_mint
docs: Yes mint — `mint::authority = mint_authority`.

accounts.name: no_mint
docs: No mint — `mint::authority = mint_authority`.

accounts.name: user_usdc
docs: User's USDC source ATA — pays the `amount` USDC for `mint_pair`.

accounts.name: user_yes
docs: User's Yes ATA. Receives the minted Yes (then immediately drained
into the Yes escrow by the market-sell up-front lock).

accounts.name: user_no
docs: User's No ATA. Receives the minted No (the user's keepsake from
this whole flow).

accounts.name: mint_authority
docs: in both the mint_pair leg and the place_order leg.

instructions.name: buy_no_limit
docs: Resting "Buy No" **limit** order (PRD §211) — mint a Yes/No pair, then
post the Yes leg as a *limit* sell: cross whatever rests at/above
`min_yes_sell_price`, then rest the remainder as the user's Yes ask.
Unlike `buy_no` it never reverts on a partial fill; the user keeps
`amount` No and the unfilled Yes rests until a buyer crosses it (or they
cancel it via `cancel_order`). Reuses the `BuyNo` accounts.

accounts.name: user
docs: Caller. Pays USDC, ends holding No tokens.

accounts.name: config
docs: Global config — paused flag + canonical USDC mint.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market the trade is against. `has_one` checks pin the Yes / No
mints stored on `Market` to the accounts the user passes.

accounts.name: book
docs: Zero-copy `Book` account for the market. Mutated by the market-sell
leg via `place_order_inner`.

accounts.name: usdc_escrow
docs: USDC escrow PDA — receives the `amount` USDC from `mint_pair_inner`,
sources the per-fill USDC payouts to makers in `place_order_inner`.

accounts.name: yes_escrow
docs: Yes escrow PDA — receives the minted Yes from the taker's
up-front lock in `place_order_inner`, sourced out to makers per fill.

accounts.name: yes_mint
docs: Yes mint — `mint::authority = mint_authority`.

accounts.name: no_mint
docs: No mint — `mint::authority = mint_authority`.

accounts.name: user_usdc
docs: User's USDC source ATA — pays the `amount` USDC for `mint_pair`.

accounts.name: user_yes
docs: User's Yes ATA. Receives the minted Yes (then immediately drained
into the Yes escrow by the market-sell up-front lock).

accounts.name: user_no
docs: User's No ATA. Receives the minted No (the user's keepsake from
this whole flow).

accounts.name: mint_authority
docs: in both the mint_pair leg and the place_order leg.

instructions.name: cancel_order
docs: Cancel a resting order by its stable [`matching::OrderKey`] `(price,
seq)`. Owner-only; refunds the escrowed collateral to the owner's
ATA.

accounts.name: user
docs: Order owner. Must equal the resting order's `owner` field.

accounts.name: usdc_escrow
docs: USDC escrow PDA. Refunds Buy-side cancels.

accounts.name: yes_escrow
docs: Yes escrow PDA. Refunds Sell-side cancels.

accounts.name: user_usdc
docs: Owner's USDC ATA. Receives the refund on Buy-side cancels.

accounts.name: user_yes
docs: Owner's Yes ATA. Receives the refund on Sell-side cancels.

instructions.name: create_strike_market
docs: Admin-only: create a strike market (Market + Book + Yes/No mints
+ USDC/Yes escrows) for a `(ticker, strike, expiry)` triple.

accounts.name: admin
docs: Admin signer. Must equal `config.admin`.

accounts.name: config
docs: Global config — used only to authenticate the admin. Boxed so the
Config struct doesn't sit inline on the SBPF function frame
alongside the SPL accounts below; growing Config (e.g. adding
`pyth_receiver`) otherwise pushes try_accounts past the 4 KB cap.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market PDA. Seed = `(ticker, strike, expiry)` so a second call for
the same triple fails the PDA derivation.

accounts.name: book
docs: Order book PDA. Zero-copy because it carries `BookSide<32>`s.
`space` is the `Book` data size plus Anchor's 8-byte discriminator.

accounts.name: yes_mint
docs: Yes-token mint. Mint authority is the per-market PDA; freeze
authority is unset (mints can't be frozen — keeps the design
simple at the cost of an option we don't need for the demo).

accounts.name: no_mint
docs: No-token mint. Same authority pattern as `yes_mint`.

accounts.name: mint_authority
docs: and the owner of the escrow token accounts below. No account data
stored at this address — the seed + bump pair *is* the identity.

accounts.name: usdc_escrow
docs: USDC escrow — receives buy-side resting collateral and any
mint_pair / settlement deposits. Owned by `mint_authority` PDA.

accounts.name: yes_escrow
docs: Yes-token escrow — receives sell-side resting Yes inventory.
Owned by the same `mint_authority` PDA.

accounts.name: usdc_mint
docs: USDC mint — must match the one pinned in `config`.

instructions.name: initialize_config
docs: Bootstrap the singleton Config PDA. First caller becomes admin.
`pyth_receiver` is the on-chain Pyth Receiver program ID that
`settle_market` will validate against — operator-set so the same
`.so` works on devnet (Pyth's real receiver), mainnet (same), and
LiteSVM tests (`MERIDIAN_PROGRAM_ID`, since fixtures mint
meridian-owned `PriceUpdateV2` accounts).

accounts.name: payer
docs: Fee payer + new admin. The first signer wins: there is no prior
Config to authenticate against, so any caller can take the slot.
Operators deploy this with a controlled keypair at bootstrap.

accounts.name: config
docs: Singleton Config PDA. `init` enforces idempotency: a second call
fails the address-derivation check inside Anchor's account-init
machinery.

accounts.name: usdc_mint
docs: USDC mint pinned at bootstrap. Mint validation (decimals, freeze
authority, supply policy) is the operator's responsibility; we
only record the pubkey here.

instructions.name: mint_pair
docs: Deposit `amount` USDC into the per-market escrow and mint `amount`
Yes + `amount` No tokens to the caller. Preserves the $1.00
invariant `yes_supply == no_supply == usdc_escrow`.

accounts.name: user
docs: Caller — pays USDC, receives Yes + No.

accounts.name: config
docs: Global config — read-only here; we only need the pause flag.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market this mint pair belongs to. The Yes/No mints and the
mint-authority PDA are validated against the fields on this account.

accounts.name: user_usdc
docs: User's USDC source ATA — must hold ≥ `amount` USDC. Mint check
happens via the `token::mint = config.usdc_mint` constraint.

accounts.name: usdc_escrow
docs: USDC escrow PDA owned by the market's mint-authority PDA. Receives
the `amount` USDC the user deposits.

accounts.name: yes_mint
docs: Yes mint — `mint::authority = mint_authority` PDA.

accounts.name: no_mint
docs: No mint — `mint::authority = mint_authority` PDA.

accounts.name: user_yes
docs: User's Yes-token ATA. Receives newly-minted Yes tokens.

accounts.name: user_no
docs: User's No-token ATA. Receives newly-minted No tokens.

accounts.name: mint_authority
docs: bump pair below; signs the two `mint_to` CPIs.

instructions.name: place_limit_order
docs: Place a price-time-priority limit order on the book. Partial fills
settle inline against opposing makers (up to
[`instructions::place_limit_order::MAX_FILLS_PER_TX`]); any residual
posts to the caller's side with the engine's next sequence number.
Maker ATAs (USDC + Yes per fill) must be supplied as
`remaining_accounts` in fill order — see the module docs for the
layout.

accounts.name: user
docs: Taker. Pays collateral up front; receives counter-asset on fill.

accounts.name: config
docs: Global config — read-only; we only need the pause flag and USDC mint.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market the book belongs to. Yes mint and settled-flag are read here.

accounts.name: book
docs: Zero-copy `Book` PDA. Loaded mutably for the match + insert.
We use the plain `AccountLoader<Book>` rather than Anchor 1.0's
`LazyAccount<Book>` because every code path in this instruction
touches both sides (`bids` and `asks`) — the deferred-deserialize
win of `LazyAccount` is marginal when the whole book is read each
step. The plan §U5 §3 explicitly endorses this choice.

accounts.name: usdc_escrow
docs: USDC escrow PDA owned by the per-market `mint_authority`.

accounts.name: yes_escrow
docs: Yes-token escrow PDA owned by the same `mint_authority`.

accounts.name: yes_mint
docs: Yes mint — only used to validate the escrow and maker ATAs.

accounts.name: user_usdc
docs: User's USDC ATA. Source for Buy bids, destination for Sell-taker
proceeds + price-improvement refunds.

accounts.name: user_yes
docs: User's Yes ATA. Source for Sell asks, destination for Buy-taker
fills.

accounts.name: mint_authority
docs: seed+bump pair below; signs every PDA-side transfer this instruction
emits.

instructions.name: place_market_order
docs: Place a market order — same matching flow as `place_limit_order`
but any unfilled residual is refunded to the taker rather than
posted. `slippage_bound` caps the worst price the taker accepts.

accounts.name: user
docs: Taker. Pays collateral up front; receives counter-asset on fill.

instructions.name: redeem
docs: Burn `amount` of the **winning** token from the caller's ATA and
PDA-signed transfer `amount` USDC from escrow back to the caller.
Requires `market.settled` + `outcome.is_some()`. Caller-supplied
`winning_mint` must match the outcome (`WrongRedeemMint` otherwise).

accounts.name: user
docs: Caller — pays winning tokens, receives USDC.

accounts.name: winning_mint
docs: The **winning** mint. We don't constrain via seeds here because the
"which side wins" is read off the market's outcome at handler time;
the handler verifies `winning_mint.key()` matches `market.{yes,no}_mint`
based on outcome. Passing the losing mint trips `WrongRedeemMint`.

accounts.name: user_winning
docs: User's ATA for the winning token. Source of the burn.

accounts.name: user_usdc
docs: User's USDC ATA. Destination of the $1-per-token payout.

accounts.name: usdc_escrow
docs: USDC escrow PDA. Source of the payout.

instructions.name: sell_no
docs: Atomic "Sell No" — market-buy `amount` Yes, then burn the freshly
bought Yes + a matching `amount` of the caller's existing No to
reclaim `amount` USDC. Reverts atomically if the Yes buy leg can't
fill the full `amount` within `max_yes_buy_price`. User's net USDC
delta is `amount - sum(fill_price * fill_qty)`.

accounts.name: user
docs: Caller. Holds `amount` No upfront, ends with USDC delta + 0 Yes.

accounts.name: user_usdc
docs: User's USDC ATA. Sources the up-front lock for the market-buy and
receives the burn_pair USDC at the end.

accounts.name: user_yes
docs: User's Yes ATA. Starts at any balance — the market-buy leg deposits
the bought Yes here, then burn_pair burns `amount` from it. Net
change: 0 (started 0, market-buy ended with `amount`, burn cleared).

accounts.name: user_no
docs: User's No ATA. Must hold ≥ `args.amount` before the call — the
burn_pair leg drains `amount` from it. Validated via Anchor
constraint so the failure surfaces before any state mutation.
Note: we reference the instruction args via `#[instruction(args:
SellNoArgs)]` so the constraint can read `args.amount` at account
validation time. Anchor 1.0 wires this through cleanly.

accounts.name: mint_authority
docs: refund and the per-fill USDC payouts.

instructions.name: set_paused
docs: Admin-only: flip the global `config.paused` kill switch. `true`
pauses every user-facing instruction; `false` resumes. The only
way to toggle the flag that `initialize_config` sets to `false`.

accounts.name: admin
docs: Admin authority. Must equal `config.admin`.

accounts.name: config
docs: Singleton Config. Seeds + bump pin it to the canonical PDA; the
`has_one` ties the signer to the recorded admin.

instructions.name: set_require_full_verification
docs: Admin-only: toggle whether `settle_market` requires a fully
Wormhole-verified (`VerificationLevel::Full`) Pyth price. Defaults to
`true` at `initialize_config`; relax on devnet if only `Partial`
updates are available.

accounts.name: admin
docs: Admin authority. Must equal `config.admin`.

accounts.name: config
docs: Singleton Config. Seeds + bump pin it to the canonical PDA; the
`has_one` ties the signer to the recorded admin.

instructions.name: settle_market
docs: Read the Pyth `PriceUpdateV2` account and stamp the market's
outcome (R15a). Public — anyone can call once the expiry is past
and the oracle is fresh enough. Atomically sets
`settled = true` + `outcome = Some(_)` so any subsequent reader
sees the invariant `settled → outcome.is_some()`.

accounts.name: caller
docs: Caller. Pays no SOL — `Market` is already initialized and we don't
open new accounts here. Doesn't need to be admin (the oracle + the
expiry timestamp are the actual gate).

accounts.name: config
docs: Global config — read for the operator-set `pyth_receiver` pubkey
that the price-update account must be owned by. Boxed for stack
hygiene (Config is ~130 bytes; inline copies inflate try_accounts).

accounts.name: market
docs: Market to settle. Mutated.

accounts.name: price_update
docs: Pyth `PriceUpdateV2` account. Validated manually in the handler:
1. `owner == config.pyth_receiver` — operator-pinned per cluster
(Pyth's real receiver on devnet/mainnet; this program's ID in
LiteSVM fixtures).
2. First 8 bytes match the vendored `PriceUpdateV2` discriminator
(Anchor `try_deserialize` enforces this).
3. The Borsh-decoded `PriceFeedMessage` survives the freshness +
feed-id + confidence checks below.
`UncheckedAccount` is required because Anchor's `Account<T>` for
types declared with `#[account]` pins the owner check to
`crate::ID`, which would reject real Pyth-owned accounts. We
reproduce the discriminator check ourselves via `try_deserialize`
after the manual owner check below.

instructions.name: settle_sweep
docs: Iteratively drain resting orders on a settled market and refund
their escrowed collateral to the owners (R15b). Public crank;
reentrant-safe across multiple calls. `max_orders = 0` is a no-op,
as is calling on an already-empty book.

accounts.name: caller
docs: Caller — anyone. Doesn't have to be admin or order owner; the
instruction is a public crank that just refunds escrowed collateral
to the resting orders' owners.

accounts.name: usdc_escrow
docs: USDC escrow PDA. Refunds bid cancellations.

accounts.name: yes_escrow
docs: Yes escrow PDA. Refunds ask cancellations.

accounts.name: yes_mint
docs: Yes mint — used to validate per-fill recipient ATAs.

errors.name: Unauthorized
errors.msg: Signer is not authorized for this instruction.

errors.name: AlreadyInitialized
errors.msg: Config is already initialized.

errors.name: ProgramPaused
errors.msg: Program is paused.

errors.name: InvalidAmount
errors.msg: Invalid amount or price (must be > 0 and within range).

errors.name: MarketSettled
errors.msg: Market is already settled; orders cannot be placed.

errors.name: MarketNotSettled
errors.msg: Market is not settled yet.

errors.name: MarketNotExpired
errors.msg: Market is not yet expired; cannot settle.

errors.name: OracleStale
errors.msg: Oracle price update is too stale.

errors.name: OracleConfidenceTooWide
errors.msg: Oracle confidence interval is too wide.

errors.name: BookFull
errors.msg: Order book side is full.

errors.name: OrderNotFound
errors.msg: Order not found on the book.

errors.name: OracleFeedIdMismatch
errors.msg: Oracle account feed id does not match the market's pinned feed id.

errors.name: WrongRedeemSide
errors.msg: Redeem called with the losing side; only the winning token is redeemable.

errors.name: WrongRedeemMint
errors.msg: Redeem-side mint does not match the market's recorded outcome.

errors.name: InvalidOraclePrice
errors.msg: Oracle returned a non-positive price; refusing to settle.

errors.name: SlippageNotMet
errors.msg: Order could not fully fill within slippage bound; revise and retry.

errors.name: InvariantBroken
errors.msg: Internal invariant violated. This is a bug — please report.

errors.name: InvalidOracleOwner
errors.msg: Oracle account is not owned by the operator-pinned Pyth receiver program.

errors.name: EmergencyGraceNotElapsed
errors.msg: Admin emergency-settle grace period has not elapsed yet.

errors.name: OracleVerificationInsufficient
errors.msg: Oracle price update is not fully verified; refusing to settle.

errors.name: MarketExpired
errors.msg: Market has reached expiry; trading is closed.

errors.name: BadMakerAccount
errors.msg: Maker payout account is not the maker's canonical associated token account.

types.name: Book
docs: Order book for a single market.
Zero-copy via Anchor's `#[account(zero_copy)]` — `Book` is `Pod` (the
hand-written impls live on `BookSide` and `OrderEntry` in the matching
module). The two sides are read/written via `AccountLoader<Book>` and
`load_mut()` at instruction time.

fields.name: market
docs: The Market PDA this book belongs to. Used as a cross-check when
instructions take both Market and Book accounts.

fields.name: bids
docs: Bid side — best (highest price) first, FIFO within a price.

fields.name: asks
docs: Ask side — best (lowest price) first, FIFO within a price.

fields.name: next_seq
docs: Monotonically-increasing sequence number, shared across both sides.
The program bumps this on every place so resting orders within a
price level keep FIFO order across cancels and partial fills.

types.name: BuyNoArgs
docs: `buy_no` arguments.
`min_yes_sell_price` is the worst (lowest) price-per-Yes the taker
will accept on the sell leg. See module docs for the naming rationale.
Pass `1` for "no floor" (`0` is rejected — collides with the OrderKey
invalid sentinel).

fields.name: amount
docs: Quantity of No tokens the user wants to end up holding.

fields.name: min_yes_sell_price
docs: Slippage floor for the Yes sell leg (microunits per Yes token).

types.name: CancelOrderArgs
docs: `cancel_order` arguments.
`side` matches `place_limit_order`'s convention: 0 = Bid, 1 = Ask.
`(price, seq)` is the full `OrderKey` the place instruction returned
(via `msg!` log) when the order was placed.

fields.name: side
docs: 0 = Bid, 1 = Ask.

fields.name: price
docs: Price half of the order's `OrderKey`.

fields.name: seq
docs: Sequence half of the order's `OrderKey`.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump for `[b"config"]`.

fields.name: paused
docs: Global pause flag — user-facing instructions refuse when set.

fields.name: admin
docs: Admin authority — currently the only privileged role
(`create_strike_market`, `pause`/`unpause`, settle override).

fields.name: fee_authority
docs: Fee authority — receives any fee accruals once fees are wired
(deferred to follow-up work; field exists for forward compatibility).

fields.name: usdc_mint
docs: USDC mint used for all collateral on this deployment. Pinned at
`initialize_config` time; markets reference it indirectly via the
program's escrow PDAs.

fields.name: pyth_receiver
docs: Pyth Receiver program ID — the expected owner for every
`PriceUpdateV2` account `settle_market` accepts. Operator sets this
at `initialize_config` time:
* Devnet / mainnet: Pyth's on-chain Receiver program
(`rec5EKMGg6MxZYaMdyBfgwp4d5rB9T1VQH5pJv5LtFJ` as of 2026).
* LiteSVM / test fixtures: this program's own ID
(`MERIDIAN_PROGRAM_ID`), because test code mints owner-meridian
accounts via `set_account`.
Pinning this in Config (rather than hardcoding the Pyth Receiver
pubkey in code) lets the same `.so` work in both environments
without a feature flag.

fields.name: require_full_verification
docs: When `true`, `settle_market` requires the Pyth price update to carry
`VerificationLevel::Full` (two-thirds of the Wormhole guardian set
signed). Secure-by-default: `initialize_config` sets this `true`, so a
fresh deployment is mainnet-correct without anyone flipping a switch.
An operator can relax it (e.g. on devnet where only `Partial` updates
are posted) via `set_require_full_verification`.

types.name: CreateStrikeMarketArgs
docs: Arguments to `create_strike_market`.
Bundled into a single struct so the PDA-seed derivation in the
`#[instruction(...)]` macro stays readable.

fields.name: ticker
docs: 8-byte right-padded ASCII ticker (e.g. `b"META\0\0\0\0"`).

fields.name: strike_price
docs: Strike price in USDC microunits.

fields.name: expiry_unix
docs: Expiry as a unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

fields.name: pyth_feed_id
docs: Pyth `PriceUpdateV2` feed id for the underlying.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump for the seed `[b"market", ticker, strike_le, expiry_le]`.

fields.name: mint_authority_bump
docs: Bump for the Yes/No mint authority PDA at
`[b"mint_auth", market.key().as_ref()]`. Cached so the mint-path
instructions (`mint_pair`, redeem) can sign without recomputing.

fields.name: settled
docs: `true` once `settle_market` has recorded an outcome.

fields.name: ticker
docs: 8-byte right-padded ASCII ticker (e.g. `b"META\0\0\0\0"`). Fixed
width so the PDA seed is stable.

fields.name: strike_price
docs: Strike price in USDC microunits (USDC has 6 decimals; $680.00 →
680_000_000). Pyth feeds are normalized to the same unit at
settle time.

fields.name: expiry_unix
docs: Expiry as a unix timestamp (seconds since epoch). The plan called
this `expiry_slot`; renamed to `expiry_unix` because Pyth +
`Clock::unix_timestamp` work in unix seconds, not Solana slots.

fields.name: yes_mint
docs: Yes-token mint pubkey. Owned by the mint-authority PDA above.

fields.name: no_mint
docs: No-token mint pubkey. Owned by the mint-authority PDA above.

fields.name: sweep_cursor
docs: Cursor into the open-order list for the iterative settle sweep
(R15b). Resumable across multiple `settle_sweep` calls so a market
with more open orders than fit in a single transaction's CU budget
can still be drained.

fields.name: outcome
docs: Settled outcome, populated by `settle_market`. `None` until then.

fields.name: pyth_feed_id
docs: Pyth `PriceUpdateV2` feed id (32-byte) for the underlying.
Pinned at market creation so settle can verify it.

types.name: Outcome
docs: Binary outcome of a settled market.
Stored as `Option<Outcome>` on [`Market`] so the pre-settle state is
unambiguous (`None`). Borsh-serializable for normal-account use.

types.name: PlaceLimitOrderArgs
docs: `place_limit_order` arguments.
`side` is encoded as `u8` (0 = Bid, 1 = Ask) because matching's `Side`
enum isn't `AnchorSerialize`. The handler maps the byte to [`Side`].

fields.name: side
docs: 0 = Bid (Buy Yes), 1 = Ask (Sell Yes). Any other value rejected.

fields.name: price
docs: Limit price in USDC microunits per Yes token.

fields.name: qty
docs: Quantity in Yes-token base units.

fields.name: side
docs: 0 = Bid (Buy Yes), 1 = Ask (Sell Yes).

fields.name: qty
docs: Quantity in Yes-token base units.

fields.name: slippage_bound
docs: Slippage bound (worst acceptable price per Yes token). For a Bid
taker this is the max it will pay; for an Ask taker the min it
will accept. `0` is rejected — pass `1` for "no floor" Ask or
`u64::MAX` for "no ceiling" Bid.

fields.name: amount
docs: Quantity of No tokens the user wants to liquidate.

fields.name: max_yes_buy_price
docs: Slippage cap for the Yes buy leg (microunits per Yes token).

fields.name: max_orders
docs: Number of resting orders to drain this call. Capped at
[`MAX_SWEEP_PER_TX`]; 0 is a no-op success.
